frameworks.name: Prospect Theory
frameworks.one_line: Behavioral economic account of decision-making under risk applied to rats.

core_question: Do rats show the same risk-sensitive choice signatures that Prospect Theory describes in humans?
key_assumptions: Choice value is shaped by probability weighting and outcome valuation
An internal reference point defines gains versus losses
Reward history can serve as that reference point
manipulations: Choice between guaranteed and probabilistic rewards
Reward probability and magnitude across trials
key_findings: Rats showed diminishing marginal sensitivity to larger rewards
Rats exhibited reference-dependent valuation tied to reward history
interpretations: Risk-sensitive choice signatures are conserved between rats and humans
Suggests shared neural substrates accessible to molecular/circuit tools in rodents
